title: Miso Sweet Corn With Togarashi Yoghurt
summary: Shichi-mi tōgarashi is one of many Japanese flavour bombs, transforming something simple into something incredible. Make a jar and use on noodles or veggies.

ingredients.label: 2 Corn On The Cob In Their Husks (Hulled Corn Without Husks Will Also Work)
ingredients.name: Corn On The Cob

ingredients.label: 3 Oranges, Peel Only (Plus An Optional Extra For Zesting)
ingredients.name: Orange

ingredients.label: 1 Tbsp Black Sesame (Plus Extra For Garnish)
ingredients.name: Black Sesame Seed

method.description: Peel your oranges carefully, trying to avoid the white pith as it’s bitter. Toast them in the oven at 100°C for 45- 55 mins or until they are totally dry. You can also remove them a bit earlier and let them finish drying out at room temperature, which might take another day (to save you some electricity).

Use a spice grinder or a good blender to grind the ingredients for the togarashi. Blitz the orange into a powder. Blitz the nori into fine flakes. Blitz the pepper flakes and sichuan peppercorns together. Pulse the sesame seeds until just broken down. Mix everything together adding the ground ginger here. 
Mix the red pepper and Sichuan peppercorns last and slowly to your taste as it can be very spicy.
Melt 3 tbsp butter and whisk with 1 tbsp miso paste.
Mix the yoghurt with 1 tbsp togarashi and the juice of half a lime and season with salt. Taste and add more togarashi if you like.
Microwave sweetcorn in the husk for 3 ½ mins. If you are using hulled corn (without husks), you can microwave them in a tupperware with a little water in the bottom and the lid slightly ajar.
To serve, brush the corn with miso butter. Smother the whole corn with yoghurt using the back of a spoon. Finish with more togarashi, black sesame seeds, chives and orange zest.

chefs.name: Christina Soteriou
chefs.summary: Christina (@christinasots) is a plant-based chef and recipe developer with a love for the Mediterranean flavours of her home, Cyprus. With an equal balance of healthy, flavourful and easy to make, there are no compromises here!
